items.title: Parris Island drill instructor found deceased in Florida hotel
items.description: Sgt. Albert Haynes II, 24, was in Florida to attend a poolee function with U.S. Marine Corps Recruiting Station-Jacksonville at the time of the incident.
items.author: J.D. Simkins

items.title: Future presence of US troops in Lithuania is ‘under review,’ says minister
items.description: U.S. troops presently stationed in the country are now leaving as expected, but the next scheduled group is not arriving, the defense minister said.
items.author: Andrius Sytas, Reuters

items.title: US airman found dead on Yokota Air Base
items.description: Airman 1st Class Sean S. Chase was discovered unresponsive on Saturday in an on-base dormitory at Yokota Air Base, Japan.
items.author: Cristina Stassis

items.title: Air Force crew honors WWII-era Flying Tigers with A-10 paint job
items.description: Maintainers at Moody AFB have transformed an A-10 into a piece of visual history, tracing its lineage to the famous Flying Tigers squadron of WWII.
items.author: Claire Barrett

items.title: Immigration concerns and the ‘very typical’ impact on military recruiting
items.description: Recruiters, attorneys and advocates say immigration questions continue to arise in conversations about military service.
items.author: Natalie Oliverio

items.title: Medal of Honor recipient Bruce Crandall, whose heroism was chronicled in ‘We Were Soldiers Once,’ dies at 93
items.description: Ret. Col. Bruce Crandall received the Medal of Honor for repeatedly flying his helicopter into intense enemy fire to evacuate dozens of wounded troops.
items.author: Claire Barrett

items.title: Philippine, U.S. Forces Conduct Maritime Cooperative Activity
items.description: The U.S. Coast Guard and the Armed Forces of the Philippines partnered for a joint maritime exercise to strengthen regional cooperation and support a free and open Indo-Pacific.
items.author: Navy Lt. Sarah Merrill, U.S. 7th Fleet

items.title: US Navy selects companies for at-sea MUSV prototype testing
items.description: The U.S. Navy announced on Friday that it had selected seven companies to compete to secure the service’s Medium Unmanned Surface Vessel contract.
items.author: Claire Barrett

items.title: Walter Reed, Other Military Hospitals Get 'A' Grade for Patient Care
items.description: Walter Reed National Military Medical Center earned an "A" hospital safety grade from Leapfrog Group for spring 2026, the fifth consecutive six-month cycle it has earned the grade.
items.author: Bernard S. Little, Walter Reed National Military Medical Center Hospital Communications

items.title: Pentagon chief sounds ‘alarm’ over China’s buildup, urges allies to boost defense spending
items.description: Hegseth urged Asian allies to ramp up military spending to counter China’s growing power.
items.author: Gregor Stuart Hunter, Rae Wee and Jun Yuan Yong, Reuters

items.title: US court blocks Pentagon from removing transgender troops, for now
items.description: The court ruled that the Trump administration could, for now, bar transgender people from enlisting, but blocked the discharge of current service members.
items.author: Daniel Wiessner, Reuters

items.title: Trump invokes national security in push for White House ballroom ‘drone port’
items.description: The president wrote that today's highly sophisticated weaponry means "we can no longer defend Washington, D.C., with rifles and pistols, alone."
items.author: Tanya Noury

items.title: Air Force returns T-38 Talon to flight status while crash investigation continues
items.description: After over a week of a fleetwide halt in operations, the T-38 Talon is returning to flying status while the May 12 crash investigation continues.
items.author: Cristina Stassis

items.title: US military seeks cultural advisors in Somalia amid regional strikes
items.description: The U.S. special operations task force in Somalia is looking for contractors to advise troops on Somali politics, culture and tribal dynamics.
items.author: Eve Sampson

items.title: 2026 MHS Conference Keynote Speaker Thanks Military Medicine for His Recovery
items.description: Medal of Honor recipient retired Army Capt. Florent Groberg shared his powerful story of combat survival, mental health recovery and leadership at the 2026 Military Health System Conference in Dallas.
items.author: Andrew Ortuzar, MHS Communications

items.title: US military continues self-defense strikes against Iran, intercepts missiles
items.description: The ceasefire that began April 12 between the U.S. and Iran is still in effect, U.S. Central Command confirmed.
items.author: Riley Ceder

items.title: Not ‘just a gun’: New SOCOM rifle allows barrel swapping and cartridge changes
items.description: At SOCOM’s request, the rifle features a swappable barrel, allowing operators to change between legacy cartridges and the newer 6.5mm Creedmoor.
items.author: Daniel Terrill

items.title: US general holds rare meeting with Cuban military officials near Guantanamo Bay
items.description: The meeting is the first in recent memory by a head of U.S. Southern Command and comes amid growing concerns in Cuba of a possible U.S. military attack.
items.author: Phil Stewart, Reuters

items.title: Hegseth Outlines U.S. Vision For Indo-Pacific at Annual Asia Defense Summit
items.description: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth discussed the strategic vision for the Indo-Pacific region, as it relates to the new National Defense Strategy, during remarks at the annual International Institute for Strategic Studies Shangri-La Dialogue 2026 in Singapore.
items.author: Matthew Olay

items.title: Air Force slashes number of fields eligible for fiscal year 2026 reenlistment bonuses
items.description: The number of career fields elibile for the Selective Retention Bonus dropped by 73% in fiscal year 2026.
items.author: Cristina Stassis

items.title: Texas Military Department Partners With Civilian Agencies for 2026 SAREX
items.description: The Texas Military Department participated in the 2026 Search and Rescue Exercise in San Antonio, May 18-21, 2026, marking 12 years of interagency partnership for this effort.
items.author: Army Capt. Jasmine Mathews, 36th Combat Aviation Brigade

items.title: Trump-class battleships should not be built until weapons technology is ready, lawmakers say
items.description: Lawmakers in the House Armed Services Committee released the fiscal 2027 National Defense Authorization Act on Tuesday.
items.author: Riley Ceder

items.title: Pentagon failed to assess impact of cuts to civilian workforce, watchdog finds
items.description: Roughly 78,000 civilian positions were eliminated in 2025 — about 10% of a workforce that originally exceeded 793,000.
items.author: Tanya Noury

items.title: ‘Scary and silencing’: Troops, families receive threats from foreign bad actors
items.description: The threats began after the U.S. strikes against Iran and have included identifying details about troops, their spouses and children.
items.author: Karen Jowers

items.title: SpaceX awarded $4 billion Space Force contract to track airborne threats
items.description: As part of the SB-AMTI program, SpaceX is meant to accelerate the delivery of sensors tracking and targeting threats globally from space.
items.author: Cristina Stassis

items.title: 24th Marine Expeditionary Unit Assumes the Watch as Littoral Combat Force-24, Advancing Operation Southern Spear in USSOUTHCOM AOR
items.description: The 24th Marine Expeditionary Unit has officially assumed the mission as the premier tactical force-in-readiness within the U.S. Southern Command Area of Responsibility. Operating under the designation of Littoral Combat Force-24, the command offers tactical options via an agile Marine Air-Ground Task Force of over 1,300 Marines and Sailors to the theater. Commanded by U.S. Marine Corps Col. Ryan Lynch, LCF-24 is directly nested under Joint Force Maritime Component Command and Joint Task Force 84-2 to support Operation Southern Spear.
items.author: Capt. Shawn Doublet

items.title: 44th Medical Brigade Integrates Drones Into Medical Resupply Operations
items.description: Soldiers assigned to the XVIII Airborne Corps' 44th Medical Brigade are expanding battlefield medical support capabilities by integrating unmanned aircraft systems into medical resupply operations.
items.author: Army Sgt. Brandon Hocson, XVIII Airborne Corps Public Affairs

items.title: National Guardsman arrested for $30 million international fraud scheme
items.description: U.S. Marshals arrested a soldier on an Army base who was wanted internationally for fraud and money-laundering resulting in over $30 million in losses.
items.author: Eve Sampson

items.title: 1,200 active-duty troops will be invited to White House UFC event
items.description: Roughly 1,200 tickets out of a total 4,000 are reserved for active members of the U.S. military.
items.author: Tanya Noury

items.title: US Navy to explore powering shore installations with aircraft carriers
items.description: Acting Navy Secretary Hung Cao revealed that Naval Station Norfolk will be “powered from an aircraft carrier" sometime this summer.
items.author: Claire Barrett

items.title: Vance advises Air Force Academy graduates to not concede decision making to AI
items.description: Vice President JD Vance warned Air Force cadets to not "submit" to AI as it continues to change warfare.
items.author: Cristina Stassis

items.title: Marine ACV crews to face ‘thinking adversaries’ in new VR simulator
items.description: A crew gunnery training prototype for the new amphibious combat vehicle will simulate an all-out fight with an unpredictable adversary.
items.author: Hope Hodge Seck

items.title: War Department Signs $9.7B Technology Deal With Dell for Microsoft Services
items.description: The War Department signed a $9.7 billion, five-year enterprisewide contract to purchase Microsoft Services, a move expected to save the department an initial $422 million annually.
items.author: C. Todd Lopez

items.title: MRF-SEA Marines and Sailors reflect on sacrifice during visit to Bataan
items.description: Exhausted, starving and surrounded, more than 75,000 American and Filipino soldiers surrendered on the Bataan Peninsula on April 9, 1942, unaware they were about to endure one of the most brutal atrocities of World War II. Forced to march nearly 65 miles under the scorching Philippine sun with little food and water, thousands died during what would become known as the Bataan Death March.
items.author: Cpl. Ryan Ramsammy

items.title: Personal Property Activity Expands PCS Support Across Joint Force
items.description: Now that peak permanent change of station season is underway, the Personal Property Activity is providing tips and operating a 24/7 call center until Sept. 15 to ensure support is available for service members and their families preparing for a PCS.
items.author: Air Force Staff Sgt. David Phaff, Personal Property Activity

items.title: Moody Air Force Base Installs Refueling Probe, Improves Efficiency
items.description: Airmen assigned to Moody Air Force Base, Ga., installed the base's first air refueling probe on an A-10C Thunderbolt II subsonic attack aircraft, enhancing its ability to support combat search and rescue and close-air support operations.
items.author: Air Force Airman 1st Class Rachel Howell

items.title: Hegseth Praises U.S. Military Efforts to Protect Nigerian Christians From ISIS
items.description: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth praised recent efforts the U.S. military has made to counter the Islamic State and its ongoing murder of Christians in Nigeria.
items.author: Matthew Olay

items.title: Sword 26: Cyber Defenders Train in Estonia, Secure Critical Infrastructure
items.description: U.S. soldiers and National Guard cyber professionals trained with members of the Estonian Defense Forces to defend the networks that modern societies rely on daily during a Sword 26 simulated exercise.
items.author: Army Capt. Victoria Connell, Multi-Domain Command – Europe

items.title: Coast Guard Commissions 62nd Fast Response Cutter Honoring 9/11 Hero
items.description: The Coast Guard commissioned its newest fast response cutter, the Vincent Danz, for official entry into its service fleet during a ceremony in New York City.
items.author: Coast Guard Chief Warrant Officer Sara Muir, Coast Guard Forces Micronesia/Sector Guam

items.title: Pennsylvania Guard Shapes Army's Extended Basic Leader Course
items.description: National Guardsmen assigned to Fort Indiantown Gap, Pa., are leading the validation effort for the Army's expanded basic leader course, refining the curriculum before it is implemented forcewide later this year.
items.author: Army Sgt. 1st Class Shane Smith, 166th Regiment - Regional Training Institute

items.title: District of Columbia National Guard Unveils Freedom 250 Patch
items.description: District of Columbia National Guard leaders introduced a new assignment patch, honoring America's 250th birthday and recognizing the National Guard's support of key events in the nation's capital.
items.author: Army Sgt. Sherald McAulay, Joint Task Force District of Columbia

items.title: Gold Star Spouse Turns Grief Into Advocacy
items.description: At Charlotte, N.C., Motor Speedway, the Coca-Cola 600 was everything it is supposed to be: horsepower, heat, noise and 600 miles of punishment. It was also remembrance, with each car carrying the name of a fallen service member.
items.author: Army Maj. Wes Shinego

items.title: On Memorial Day, Commander in Chief, Secretary of War, Honor Nation's Fallen
items.description: The U.S. will soon mark its semiquincentennial — 250 years as a nation. It's a big milestone, said President Donald J. Trump, but not one that could have been achieved without more than 1 million service members having given their lives to the nation.
items.author: C. Todd Lopez

items.title: Hegseth Tells West Point Cadets They Are Ready to Lead
items.description: Speaking at Michie Stadium in West Point, N.Y., Secretary of War Pete Hegseth assured the 2026 U.S. Military Academy graduates that they have both his and the president's trust.
items.author: C. Todd Lopez

items.title: For Old Guard Soldiers, 'Flags In' Is a Personal Mission
items.description: Soldiers assigned to the 3rd U.S. Infantry Regiment honored America's fallen heroes ahead of Memorial Day by placing a U.S. flag in front of each headstone and columbarium column at Arlington National Cemetery and at the United States Soldiers' and Airmen's Home National Cemetery.
items.author: Courtesy of Arlington National Cemetery

items.title: War Department's 'Patriot Pipeline' Flows Talent to Arsenal of Freedom
items.description: While testifying before the Senate Armed Services Committee's personnel subcommittee, Anthony J. Tata, the undersecretary of war for personnel and readiness, said the department has established Project Patriot Pipeline, an initiative to unify dozens of training and workforce development programs.
items.author: C. Todd Lopez

items.title: Top Special Ops Civilian Recounts Successes, Lays Out Vision for Future
items.description: Derrick Anderson, assistant secretary of war for special operations and low-intensity conflict, closed out the Special Operations Forces Week 2026 convention by underscoring the SOF community's recent successes and articulating his plan to prioritize five areas.
items.author: Matthew Olay

items.title: Special Ops Conference Discusses Optimizing Human Performance
items.description: During the Special Operations Forces Week convention, experts stressed that early health checks, artificial intelligence tracking and family support are vital to protect operators before problems worsen.
items.author: Matthew Olay

items.title: Give Them Wings, Teach Them to Fly: The Making of a Phoenix Raven
items.description: Airmen must push through a demanding 28-day qualification course to become one of the Phoenix Ravens, a force tasked with safeguarding aircraft, personnel and critical assets in austere locations where threats may be elevated or unpredictable.
items.author: Jewaun McElroy, Joint Base McGuire-Dix-Lakehurst
items.category: GIVE THEM WINGS, TEACH THEM TO FLY

items.title: Symbol of Grit Returns, 10th Mountain Division to Wear Crossed Ski Insignia
items.description: Soldiers assigned to the 10th Mountain Division can once again wear the division's historic crossed ski insignia on their Army Green Service Uniform garrison caps, restoring a visual link to the unit's World War II roots and reinforcing the alpine spirit that resonates across the formation.
items.author: Army Maj. Geoffrey Carmichael

items.title: H-1s Become Drone Control Platforms
items.description: In a significant leap forward for aerial warfare, the U.S. Marine Corps has successfully integrated
items.author: 2ndLt Connor Jenig

items.title: Retired Combat Vet Rejoins Marine Corps
items.description: Marine Corps Staff Sgt. Johnny "Joey" Jones reenlisted in the Marine Corps Reserve at the Pentagon, 14 years after being medically retired following the loss of both legs in a 2010 Afghanistan combat deployment.
items.author: C. Todd Lopez

items.title: Senior DOW Tech Official Says Department AI Use Up 1,775% in Past Year
items.description: Emil Michael, undersecretary of war for research and engineering and War Department chief technology officer, said that use of artificial intelligence across the department is up by roughly 1.42 million users over the past calendar year.
items.author: Matthew Olay

items.title: Senior Special Ops Leaders Discuss Value of Partnerships, Alliances
items.description: Senior military special operations forces leaders from the U.S., Sweden and the Philippines participated in a panel about the importance of partnerships and alliances, as part of Special Operations Forces Week 2026 in Tampa, Fla.
items.author: Matthew Olay

items.title: War Department's Science Enterprise Maintains Tight Focus on Half-Dozen Key Areas
items.description: Emil G. Michael, undersecretary of defense for research and engineering and War Department chief technology officer, shared his plan to guarantee U.S. troops always have the tools to win before the Senate Armed Services Committee in Washington.
items.author: C. Todd Lopez

items.title: JIATF 401 Drone Defense Marketplace Broadens Allied Access to Counter-Drone Capabilities
items.description: Army Secretary Dan Driscoll and key leaders from Australia, Poland and South Korea recently signed agreements enabling each country to procure counter-small UAS technologies through the Joint Interagency Task Force 401 drone defense marketplace.
items.author: U.S. Army

items.title: Fort Bragg Honors the Fallen During Annual Remembrance Run
items.description: More than 2,000 soldiers, families and community members came together at Fort Bragg, N.C., for the annual Run, Honor, Remember 5K, an event dedicated to honoring service members who gave their lives in defense of the nation.
items.author: Jason Ragucci, Fort Bragg Garrison Public Affairs Office

items.title: Senior Leaders Discuss Recruiting STEM Candidates Into Special Ops
items.description: During a panel discussion at the Special Operations Forces Week 2026 convention in Tampa, Fla., a pair of senior SOF leaders discussed how to bring more recruits with science, technology, engineering and mathematics backgrounds into their formations.
items.author: Matthew Olay

items.title: Special Ops Convention Convenes Under 'Peace Through Strength' Strategy
items.description: More than 29,000 members of the military's special operations forces community and the public converged in person on Tampa, Fla., and virtually to kick off Special Operations Forces Week 2026.
items.author: Matthew Olay

items.title: National Defense Strategy Prioritizes America First, Leader Says
items.description: The U.S. will prioritize theaters with the greatest consequence for national interests and where only American power can play a decisive role, said Daniel Zimmerman, assistant secretary of war for international security affairs, who spoke during a hearing before the House Armed Services Committee.
items.author: David Vergun

items.title: Coast Guard's Newest Polar Icebreaker Returns Home Following Arctic Deployment
items.description: The Coast Guard cutter Storis completed a 36-day Bering Sea deployment to test its capabilities and train for high-risk Arctic missions.
items.author: U.S. Coast Guard Pacific Area

items.title: Defeating the Swarm: Project Flytrap Accelerates NATO's Counter-Drone Lethality
items.description: Project Flytrap 5.0, a U.S. Army V Corps counter-unmanned aerial systems initiative including British partner forces, integrated and tested more than 50 industry-provided technologies to fight and defeat the low-cost drones reshaping the modern battlefield.
items.author: Army Sgt. Max Elliott, U.S. Army V Corps

items.title: Joint Interagency Task Force 401 Awards $500 Million Counter-UAS Contract
items.description: JIATF 401 awarded a three-year contract with a $500 million ceiling to support enterprisewide counter-unmanned aerial system operations, ensuring that top-tier technologies can be rapidly scaled and sustained in response to evolving threats.
items.author: Army Lt. Col. Adam Scher, Joint Interagency Task Force 401

items.title: At Fort Campbell, Hegseth Underscores Significance of Purple Hearts, Enlistments
items.description: During a visit with the 101st Airborne Division at Fort Campbell, Ky., as part of its "Week of the Eagles" celebration, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth administered the oath of enlistment to a handful of screaming eagle soldiers and pinned Purple Heart medals to the chests of nine others.
items.author: C. Todd Lopez

items.title: Marine Corps Enhances ACV Driver Training with Advanced Simulation Systems
items.description: The Marine Corps is advancing how it trains Amphibious Combat Vehicle (ACV) operators by integrating
items.author: Joel Rivera-Camacho

items.title: Army Advances Barracks Modernization Efforts to Improve Quality of Life
items.description: The Army has accelerated efforts to strengthen quality-of-life investments across installations, showing a long-term commitment to ensuring soldiers live and work in facilities that reflect the professionalism, standards and readiness expectations of the service.
items.author: Elijah Cornish, Army

items.title: Fueling the Fight: USNS Kanawha Completes Strategic Deployment
items.description: Family and friends gathered on the pier at Naval Station Norfolk, Va., to welcome home the crew of the USNS Kanawha. The fleet replenishment oiler and its 92 civil service mariners returned after a 204-day deployment, including 156 days at sea.
items.author: LaShawn Sykes

items.title: High-Pressure Field Artillery Creates Bonds Between Soldiers
items.description: Behind the massive firepower of field artillery are tight-knit soldiers driven by deep trust and family-like bonds.
items.author: Army Pfc. Alexis Fischer, 22nd Mobile Public Affairs Detachment

items.title: 7th Communication Battalion Marines sustain command and control during Kaiju Rain 26
items.description: CAMP HANSEN, Okinawa, Japan—Marines with 7th Communication Battalion, III Marine Expeditionary Force Information Group, established a communication node at Camp Shields after displacing from Marine Corps Air Station Futenma during Kaiju Rain 26, May 8, 2026.
items.author: 2nd Lt. Lea Pohalski

items.title: Hegseth Welcomes Ford Carrier Strike Group Home After Record-Breaking Deployment
items.description: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth was on hand with his wife, Jennifer, and numerous senior Navy leaders at Naval Station Norfolk, Va., to welcome home sailors assigned to USS Gerald R. Ford Carrier Strike Group after a record-setting, multi-month deployment.
items.author: Matthew Olay

items.title: Pentagon Marks Armed Forces Day With Special Flag
items.description: The War Department celebrated Armed Forces Day with a newly unveiled America 250 version of the Betsy Ross flag, unfurled over the outside of the Pentagon.
items.author: David Vergun

items.title: Future USS Cleveland Sailors Attend Commissioning Week Events
items.description: Sailors assigned to the future USS Cleveland, the 16th and final Freedom-variant littoral combat ship to be built and commissioned for the Navy, arrived in their namesake city to launch a week of community engagement and celebration leading up to the ship's commissioning.
items.author: Navy Lt. j.g. Rachael Jones, Commander, Naval Surface Force

items.title: Africom Commander Says Continent at 'Crossroads' of Global Commerce, Security
items.description: Air Force Gen. Dagvin R.M. Anderson, commander of U.S. Africa Command, told Congress that as Africa ascends on the world stage, it faces a precarious situation due to bad actors operating throughout the region.
items.author: Matthew Olay

items.title: Centcom Commander Says Epic Fury Crippled Iran, Enhanced Military Partnerships in Region
items.description: Since the launch of Operation Epic Fury, American forces in the U.S. Central Command area of operations crippled Iran's military and its ability to project power, according to War Department leaders.
items.author: C. Todd Lopez

items.title: Air Force Pilots Receive Distinguished Flying Cross for Midnight Hammer Heroics
items.description: Six F-16 Fighting Falcon pilots assigned to the 55th Fighter Squadron received the Distinguished Flying Cross for their heroic actions in combat operations during a ceremony at Shaw Air Force Base, S.C.
items.author: Air Force Senior Airman Nyanda Walker-Potts

items.title: Senior Leaders Update Congress on Special Ops Community
items.description: Four of the most senior leaders from the U.S. military's special operations forces community appeared before the Senate Armed Services Committee in Washington to provide an update on the SOF components within their respective branches of service.
items.author: Matthew Olay

items.title: Army, Forest Service Complete Annual Prescribed Burn in Hawaii
items.description: U.S. Army Garrison Hawaii and the U.S. Forest Service successfully completed the annual prescribed burn of the training area at Schofield Barracks, Hawaii, treating approximately 1,707 acres of invasive vegetation to reduce wildfire risk and maintain the integrity of military training lands.
items.author: Nathan Wilkes, U.S. Army Garrison Hawaii

items.title: Quantico Hosts Inaugural Presidential Fitness Test for Military Installations
items.description: MARINE CORPS BASE QUANTICO, Va.—Over 100 Crossroads Elementary School and Quantico Middle High School students participated in MCB Quantico’s Presidential Fitness Test at Butler Stadium, April 12.
items.author: Shaehmus Sawyer

items.title: Certified! MRF-D 26 is Darwin’s first-ever Special Purpose Marine Air-Ground Task Force
items.description: Under the Palawan sun, in the austerity and humidity of Balabac, Philippines, U.S. Marines and Sailors assigned to I and III Marine Expeditionary Forces completed the final rigorous requirements for Marine Rotational Force – Darwin 26 to be certified as a Special Purpose Marine Air-Ground Task Force, May 3, 2026.
items.author: Gunnery Sgt. Nathanael Carberry

items.title: Department of the Navy Releases Fiscal Year 2027 Shipbuilding Plan
items.description: The Department of the Navy today released its Fiscal Year 2027 Shipbuilding Plan, outlining the Administration's long-term strategy to expand fleet capacity, strengthen the maritime industrial base, and deliver a more capable, ready, and lethal naval force. =
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: U.S. Marine Corps Strengthens Arctic Readiness with Campaign - Alaska
items.description: The United States Marine Corps has launched “Campaign - Alaska," a strategic initiative to enhance
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Balikatan 2026: Combined Civil-Military Team Strengthens Communities Across the Philippines
items.description: Across five humanitarian and civic assistance sites in the Philippines, U.S. service members, Armed Forces of the Philippines personnel, local leaders, barangay rural health workers and community members worked together during Exercise Balikatan 2026 to strengthen community resilience through health care, education support, infrastructure projects and civil-military engagements.
items.author: 1st Lt. Brandon Daniels

items.title: Blount Island Enables Rapid MEU Ship-to-Shore Operations, Reinforcing SOUTHCOM Readiness
items.description: U.S. Marines with the 22nd Marine Expeditionary Unit (Special Operations Capable) landed an up-armored wrecker May 5 at Marine Corps Support Facility Blount Island in Florida, demonstrating how the service rapidly moves equipment to support operations across the region.
items.author: Dustin Senger

items.title: Balikatan 2026: MRF‑D 26, joint, allied forces simulate maritime security on Balabac Island
items.description: BALABAC ISLAND, Philippines — U.S. Marines with Marine Rotational Force – Darwin 26, a now-certified Special‑Purpose Marine Air‑Ground Task Force assigned to rotational deployments in the South Pacific, participated in a live-fire assault to secure and hold Balabac Airfield in a demonstration of multi-domain interoperability alongside U.S. Soldiers from 25th Infantry Division, Philippine marines and Australian soldiers during Exercise Balikatan 2026, May 3.
items.author: 1st Lt. Chase Fortier

items.title: Training Command Updates Reconnaissance Training
items.description: Training Command launched Ground Reconnaissance Course as part of an updated Marine Reconnaissance training curriculum at Marine Corps Base Camp Pendleton, April 27. GRC is now the initial course in the updated curriculum, which replaces Basic Reconnaissance Course with two new courses: GRC and Amphibious Reconnaissance Course. This change comes from analysis of reconnaissance training and support to Force Design initiatives, including Scout Platoons and multidomain operations.
items.author: Capt. Colton Martin

items.title: Marine Corps Championships at MCB Quantico
items.description: Weapons Training Battalion–Quantico and the Marine Corps Shooting Team hosted the Marine Corps Championships, the culminating event of the Marine Corps Marksmanship Competitions, from April 13–24 at Marine Corps Base Quantico, Virginia.
items.author: Marine Corps Staff Sgt. Claudia Nix, Marine Corps Training and Education Command

items.title: VMFA-251 pioneers new F-35 communications tactics at WTI
items.description: Marines with Marine Fighter Attack Squadron (VMFA) 251 advanced F-35 and other fixed-wing aircraft ground operations by integrating improved communication and digital interoperability capabilities. They demonstrated these advancements during Weapons and Tactics Instructors course 2-26 at Marine Aviation Weapons and Tactics Squadron One (MAWTS-1), Marine Corps Air Station, Yuma, Arizona, on April 13, 2026.
items.author: Lance Cpl. Fabian Ortiz

items.title: Balikatan 2026: U.S. Marines with MRF-D 26 conduct counter-landing live-fire exercise
items.description: U.S. Marines with Marine Rotational Force – Darwin participated in a combined counter-landing live-fire exercise alongside service members from the Armed Forces of the Philippines, Australia Defence Force and New Zealand Defence Force, April 27, 2026.
items.author: 1st Lt. Chase Fortier

items.title: 3rd MLR Joins Most Expansive Exercise Balikatan To Date
items.description: 3rd Marine Littoral Regiment is contributing more than 1,300 Hawaii-based Marines and Sailors for the 41st iteration of Exercise Balikatan, an expansive multilateral exercise conducted by the Armed Forces of the Philippines and the U.S. military across the Philippine archipelago from April 20 to May 8.
items.author: 1st Lt. Duncan Stoner

items.title: Marine Corps to Unveil Next-Gen Warfighting Tech and Celebrate 250 Years of Heritage at Modern Day Marine 2026
items.description: Fresh off the celebration of the Marine Corps' own 250th anniversary last year, the service is now turning its attention to the future of expeditionary warfare as the United States prepares to mark its semi-quincentennial.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marine Corps Completes the Early Capability Release of OPF-Light
items.description: In a pivotal step for infantry modernization, the Marine Corps has successfully completed Initial Operational Test and Evaluation (IOT&E) and the early capability release of the Organic Precision Fires-Light (OPF-L) system.
items.author: Addi Vander Velde, Office of Public Affairs & Communication

items.title: 2nd MLG Leaders Visit Blount Island to Examine Prepositioning, Logistics Integration
items.description: U.S. Marine Corps Brig. Gen. Maura Hennigan, commanding general of 2nd Marine Logistics Group, led dozens of senior logistics leaders in touring the hub of service-managed, forward-positioned equipment April 15 at Marine Corps Support Facility Blount Island, Florida.
items.author: Dustin Senger

items.title: A medal, a memory, a Marine: The last flight of Maj. Everette McPherson
items.description: Nearly six decades after his aircraft went down over the South China Sea, Maj. Everett McPherson’s courage in the skies has finally been recognized. In a ceremony filled with solemn reflection and pride, the Marine Corps posthumously awarded McPherson the Distinguished Flying Cross, one of the nation’s highest decorations for heroism in aerial flight.
items.author: Lance Cpl. Claire Cheney
items.category: A MEDAL, A MEMORY, A MARINE

items.title: Department of the Navy Creates Portfolio Aqcuisition Executive-Marine Corps to Accelerate Modernization
items.description: On Apr 10, the Marine Corps took a historic step in its modernization journey with the establishment of the Portfolio Acquisition Executive-Marine Corps (PAE-MC). This new organization replaces Program Executive Office Land Systems (PEO-LS) and incorporates programs from Marine Corps Systems Command (MCSC), marking a significant transformation in how the Marine Corps develops and delivers capabilities to its warfighters.
items.author: Cathleen Close

items.title: Blount Island Command Team Supports First-Ever MPF Offload in Mindanao
items.description: A Marine Corps technical assistance and advisory team deployed in March from Jacksonville, Florida, in support of a first-of-its-kind maritime prepositioning force offload in the Indo-Pacific region.
items.author: Dustin Senger

items.title: Balikatan 26: U.S. Marines, Sailors Conduct Distributed Maritime Logistics in the Philippines
items.description: Logistics is the pacing function of expeditionary operations. Before a rifleman can eliminate a target, an infantry company can secure an objective, or the Marine Corps can train shoulder-to-shoulder with allies and partners, sustainment and equipment must be readily available at the right place and right time.
items.author: 1st Lt. Benjamin Gillman

items.title: Come to Fight, Come to Win: II MEF First to Field New OSCAR Gen IV Training
items.description: Leading the way for the Marine Corps, II Marine Expeditionary Force (II MEF) puts new doctrine into action by becoming the first command to implement the modernized Operational Stress Control and Readiness (OSCAR) training, certifying 30 new Core Master Trainers from March 24-26, 2026.
items.author: 2nd Lt. Sydney DiStefano
items.category: COME TO FIGHT, COME TO WIN

items.title: Motor Transport Unveils Integration Lab, Driving Tactical Wheeled Vehicle Modernization
items.description: Previously, Marine Corps program offices integrating new systems onto the Joint Light Tactical Vehicle faced significant logistical challenges. The process involved requesting a vehicle from Program Executive Officer Land Systems’ (PEO LS) Program Manager Motor Transport (PM Motor-T), costly and complex vehicle shipments, and potential roadblocks to project deadlines because of vehicle availability constraints. This system created inefficiencies and drove up costs.
items.author: Helena Yared

items.title: From Workshop to Waterway: Marine Innovation Redefines Naval Strike Capabilities
items.description: Marines assigned to the III Expeditionary Operations Training Group at Okinawa, Japan, and U.S. Naval Special Warfare Command operators executed the Marine Corps' first live-fire drone strike against a maritime surface vessel from a naval surface craft.
items.author: Marine Corps 1st Lt. Tyler Thomas

items.title: Marine Rotational Force – Southeast Asia continues Alliance with Philippines
items.description: The 15th Marine Expeditionary Unit concluded its rotation with Marine Rotational Force – Southeast Asia on March 31, after enhancing combined readiness with the Armed Forces of the Philippines and regional partners in Indonesia, Malaysia, Brunei, and Thailand. Building on that success, a new command element from I Marine Expeditionary Force arrived to continue the mission with a focused effort in the Philippines, integrating Marine Corps forces in support of Task Force – Philippines and strengthening the U.S.-Philippines Alliance.
items.author: Capt. MacKenzie Margroum

items.title: Closing the Sensor-to-Shooter Gap, One Dynamis Serial at a Time
items.description: In an increasingly complex global landscape, the speed of decision-making on the battlefield is not just an advantage, it is the ultimate arbiter of victory.
items.author: Courtesy Story

items.title: Closing the Sensor-to-Shooter Gap, One Dynamis Serial at a Time
items.description: In an increasingly complex global landscape, the speed of decision-making on the battlefield is not just an advantage, it is the ultimate arbiter of victory.
items.author: Courtesy Story

items.title: One Year of 12th LCT: U.S. Marines Execute a Combined-Arms Live-Fire Exercise in the Republic of Korea
items.description: U.S. Marines with 12th Littoral Combat Team, 12th Marine Littoral Regiment, 3rd Marine Division, executed company-size attacks during a Combined-Arms Live-Fire Exercise at Rodriguez Live-Fire Complex, Republic of Korea, Feb. 26 to March 4, 2026. During the company-level exercise, Marines integrated organic and supporting fires, maneuver elements, and reconnaissance capabilities to conduct a coordinated assault on a designated objective.
items.author: Cpl. Rodney Frye

items.title: MARCORSYSCOM Provides New Equipment Training
items.description: In a future conflict against peer adversaries, logistics may determine how long U.S. forces can remain in the fight. To counter supply chain vulnerabilities, the Marine Corps is pushing advanced manufacturing capabilities forward, allowing Marines to produce critical repair parts at the point of need.
items.author: Kristiana Montanez

items.title: 13th MEU Forms Complete Marine Air-Ground Task Force
items.description: The 13th Marine Expeditionary Unit (MEU) has officially formed as a complete Marine Air-Ground Task Force with the addition of its major subordinate elements, bringing the MEU’s full capability together under one command.
items.author: Capt. Mark McDonough

items.title: CORE26 | German-French Air Squadron Transports U.S. Marine Rifle Company for First Time in NATO History
items.description: In a historic demonstration of NATO’s expanding capabilities, a rifle company of U.S. Marines were airlifted in the Arctic Circle by a unique, jointly operated, French and German air transport squadron, March 11, marking the first time the squadron has ever transported United States Marines.
items.author: 1st Lt. Stephen Sewell

items.title: CORE26 | U.S. Marine Officer Walks in his Grandfather’s WWII Footsteps
items.description: KJELLER AIR BASE, NORWAY –In 1943, the U.S. Eighth Air Force dropped over 100 bombs on Kjeller Air Base to liberate it from Nazi-occupation. Among the aircrew was Bombardier-Navigator Maj. Velton Winn Cason, flying a B-24 Liberator with one mission: to break the enemy’s hold on Norway.
items.author: Capt. Jorin Hollenbeak

items.title: From MRAP to MADIS: Legacy of Innovation Endures at NIWC Atlantic
items.description: Throughout 2025, NIWC Atlantic delivered the MADIS drone-defense system to the Marine Corps. Leveraging lessons from the MRAP era, engineers used rapid prototyping and modular design to integrate advanced weapons and sensors onto JLTVs. This streamlined approach accelerates the fielding of lethal, connected technology to the modern warfighter.
items.author: Steve Ghiringhelli, NIWC Atlantic Public Affairs Office

items.title: U.S. SPACE COMMAND RECOGNITION
items.description: COLORADO SPRINGS, CO — Two Marines from Marine Corps Forces Space Command (MARFORSPACE) were honored at the U.S. Space Command’s annual Apollo Awards during a ceremony held on February 19, 2026. Maj. Austin J. Oubre and Sgt. James Zsoka were recognized for their contributions to space operations, technology development, and their direct support to U.S. Space Command (USSPACECOM).
items.author: Gunnery Sgt. Jestin Costa

items.title: Lance Cpl. Schule Designs 3D Printed Antenna Mast, Saves Fleet Marine Force Thousands
items.description: MARINE CORPS BASE CAMP LEJEUNE, N.C. — A U.S. Marine places a broken Mobile User Object System (MOUS) on a shelf, the fiberglass of the MOUS antenna mast brittle and weakened from use and exposure. It sits next to dozens of others, all unusable because of one flaw.
items.author: Lance Cpl. Franco Lewis

items.title: Exercise Panamanian Partnership 26
items.description: “For me, it always comes back to discipline and mastering the fundamentals because if we are not consistent in the basics, nothing else we do will hold up under pressure,” said U.S. Marine Corps 1st Sgt. Lonnie D. Stowers, the senior enlisted leader of Charlie Company, 2nd Combat Engineer Battalion, 2nd Marine Division.
items.author: Sgt. Antonino Mazzamuto

items.title: An Ecuadorian Marine’s Path to the U.S. Marine Corps
items.description: Long before earning the title of United States Marine, Lance Cpl. Juan G. Ipialesparedes served as a special operations Marine in his native country of Ecuador. Today, as a personnel administration specialist with 1st Marine Logistics Group, I Marine Expeditionary Force, he brings a lifetime of military experience and a deep respect for service that transcends nationality to the United States.
items.author: 2nd LT Steven Maturino

items.title: Marine Corps revises body composition standards
items.description: The Marine Corps is replacing the current semiannual height and weight evaluation with a waist-to-height ratio (WHtR) methodology as the first step of its body composition evaluations in accordance with recent Secretary of War guidance.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Beneath Norway's Mountains
items.description: Above the Arctic Circle, winter dominates the landscape. Snow blankets the mountains and subzero winds cut across frozen valleys. For most people, this terrain can feel remote and inhospitable. However, for U.S. Marines alongside NATO Allies and partners, it is a proving ground. In the Arctic, the environment can be as lethal as any adversary. This reality makes joint readiness, training, and the ability to rapidly mobilize equipment and personnel to crisis not optional, but essential to operational success in the event of armed conflict.
items.author: 1st Lt. Jorin Hollenbeak

items.title: Marine Forces Korea Sergeant Major Awarded Republic of Korea Prime Minister Commendation
items.description: In a ceremony reflecting the strength of the ROK–U.S. alliance, a senior U.S. Marine enlisted leader
items.author: Story by Gunnery Sgt. Antonio Campbell

items.title: 317th AW leads Spring 2026 C-130 WSC, integrates with I MEF
items.description: The 317th Airlift Wing led the Spring 2026 C-130 Weapon System Council (WSC) at Marine Corps Base Camp Pendleton, California, coordinating with I Marine Expeditionary Force to further integration of rapid air mobility concepts and strengthen Joint tactical airlift capabilities across the Joint and Total Force, Feb. 4-5, 2026.
items.author: Senior Airman Jade M. Caldwell

items.title: 2026 Marine Corps Aviation Plan
items.description: The Marine Corps Aviation Plan (AVPLAN) outlines the strategic vision, key capabilities, and future direction for Marine Corps Aviation, aiming to enhance readiness and address emerging threats. The 2026 AVPLAN marks a significant evolution from 2025, transitioning from strategic direction to concrete, data-driven implementation.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Shoot for the Stars
items.description: T-minus 3, 2, 1 … We have liftoff! The ground rumbled as the engines roared to life. Smoke and
items.author: Sgt. Emily De La Torre

items.title: Marine Corps Passes FY25 Financial Audit
items.description: For the third year in a row, independent auditors verified that the Marine Corps’ financial records are materially accurate, complete, and compliant with federal regulations and issued an unmodified opinion for Fiscal Year 2025.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Project Dynamis Accelerates Development of Next Generation Battle Management Command and Control
items.description: Project Dynamis, a bold Marine Corps plan to accelerate the delivery of AI-powered decision advantage to the tactical edge, has kicked off its "Dynamis Series," a months-long campaign of iterative agile software development sprints referred to as "Serials."
items.author: Courtesy Story

items.title: Onboard USS Makin Island, 13th MEU and PHIBRON 7 Strengthen Navy-Marine Corps Integration During Amphibious Training
items.description: The Navy-Marine Corps team has concluded Quarterly Underway Amphibious Readiness Training (QUART) 26.2, which took place from Jan. 20-30, 2026. The exercise, led by the 13th Marine Expeditionary Unit (MEU) and Amphibious Squadron (PHIBRON) 7, featured the Wasp-class amphibious assault ship USS Makin Island (LHD 8), which expanded the training's ability to prepare MEUs and Amphibious Ready Groups (ARGs) for future deployments and operations afloat. Further strengthening international partnerships, Peruvian naval officers were aboard to observe the exercise, fostering shared learning and military cooperation.
items.author: Capt. Mark McDonough

items.title: 2nd Marine Logistics Group 3D printed Drone
items.description: Sgt. Henry David Volpe, an automotive maintenance technician with 2nd Marine Logistics Group, has successfully developed HANX, the Marine Corps’ first NDAA-compliant, 3D-printed drone platform. Developed at the II MEF Innovation Campus in just 90 days, the HANX drone represents a major leap in tactical manufacturing by providing a low-cost, modular system that is resilient against adversary software. By utilizing 3D printing and off-the-shelf parts that meet rigorous security standards, Sgt. Volpe and his team have moved drone production from external contractors directly into the hands of Marines. This "by Marines, for Marines" innovation aligns with the Department of War’s "Drone Dominance" initiative, offering a versatile tool for reconnaissance or one-way attack missions that can be built and modified anywhere in the fleet.
items.author: Story by Lance Cpl. Javier Santillan

items.title: 2nd Marine Logistics Group; Innovating the Future of Mobile Satellite Communications
items.description: Innovation is the process of turning limitations into opportunities. When existing equipment failed to meet operational demands, U.S. Marines with 2nd Marine Logistics Group, II Marine Expeditionary Force, used ingenuity, technical skill, and mission-first thinking to develop a custom StarShield Satellite mount for Joint Light Tactical Vehicles, increasing flexibility and connectivity in austere environments.
items.author: Cpl. Jessica Mazzamuto

items.title: 3rd Maintenance Battalion Increase ACV Readiness in Okinawa
items.description: Marines and contractors with 3rd Maintenance Battalion, 3rd Marine Logistics Group, have established yet another example of Marine Corps ingenuity and adaptability, this time in the repair and maintenance of the new Amphibious Combat Vehicles.
items.author: Gunnery Sgt. Jonathan Wright

items.title: 31st MEU | U.S. Marines, Sailors Honor Legacy of Operation Swift During Battlefield Visit in Vietnam
items.description: From January 9–11, 2026, U.S. Marines and Sailors assigned to the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit (MEU) conducted a battlefield visit to the site of Operation Swift, a pivotal engagement of the Vietnam War, to honor the legacy of those who fought there and to reflect on the enduring bonds between past and present generations of service members. During the Vietnam War, Marine forces conducted amphibious and helicopter-borne operations as part of Special Landing Force Alpha, including during Operation Swift, employing integrated air, ground, and logistics elements in combat that reinforced the MAGTF construct and helped shape the MEU’s role as a forward-deployed, rapid-response force.
items.author: 1st Lt. Heather Born, III Marine Expeditionary Force

items.title: Cold Response: 25,000 NATO Allies Launch High-North Exercise
items.description: Cold Response 26 is a routine Norwegian-led winter military exercise in northern Norway. U.S. forces are participating as part of U.S. government efforts to support Norwegian and NATO defense concepts and to enable European allies to position NATO for success and effective deterrence. This exercise is part of a regular training schedule designed to enhance collective defense capabilities and ensure U.S. readiness to operate in challenging conditions. The exercise will bring together over 25,000 personnel from a dozen nations and organizations, including Canada, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, Spain, Sweden, United Kingdom, the United States and NATO to enhance interoperability and prepare for future challenges in the Arctic environment.
items.author: Master Sgt. Scott Schmidt

items.title: Tripoli Expeditionary Strike Group operates in 7th Fleet
items.description: The Tripoli Expeditionary Strike Group, composed of the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit (MEU), America-class amphibious assault ship USS Tripoli (LHA 7), Ticonderoga-class guided-missile cruiser USS Robert Smalls (CG 62) and Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer USS Rafael Peralta (DDG 115), is conducting routine operations in the U.S. 7th Fleet area of operations, Dec. 11.
items.author: Courtesy Story

items.title: Ready, Engaged, Lethal: I Marine Expeditionary Force Year in Review
items.description: From the littorals of the Indo-Pacific to the familiar training grounds of Southern California, 2025
items.author: Story by Capt. Gregory Dreibelbis

items.title: USMC Honors the life of General Michael J. Williams, 28th Assistant Commandant of the Marine Corps
items.description: The United States Marine Corps honored the life and service of the 28th Assistant Commandant of the Marine Corps, General Michael J. Williams, USMC (Ret.), with a graveside burial with full military honors on Thursday, December 18, 2025, at the National Memorial Cemetery at Quantico.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marine Corps announces updated physical fitness standards
items.description: The Marine Corps is implementing changes to its Physical Fitness Test and body composition standards in accordance with the Secretary of War’s Military Fitness Standards memorandum issued on Sept. 30, 2025.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: NAVAIR Releases V-22 Comprehensive Review Findings
items.description: Naval Air Systems Command (NAVAIR) released the findings of a comprehensive review Dec. 12, 2025, on the V-22 Osprey aircraft flown by the Air Force, Marine Corps and Navy.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Death of General Michael J. Williams, 28th Assistant Commandant of the Marine Corps
items.description: The United States Marine Corps announces the passing of General Michael J. Williams, USMC (Ret.), 28th Assistant Commandant of the Marine Corps, who died peacefully on December 6, 2025.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Above, Below, and Beyond: The Department of the Navy Honors 250 Years of the U.S. Navy and Marine Corps this Veterans Day
items.description: This Veterans Day, the Department of the Navy commemorates 250 years of American seapower with “Above, Below, and Beyond,” a two-hour Presidential special airing Sunday, Nov. 9, 2025, on Fox Nation.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marine Corps Lays Out Aggressive Modernization Efforts in Force Design Update
items.description: The Commandant of the Marine Corps, Gen. Eric M. Smith, published the 2025 Force Design Update, which lays out how the Marine Corps is aggressively pursuing modernization initiatives to ensure it remains a globally responsive, naval expeditionary force.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marines Showcase Amphibious Capabilities for Marine Corps’ 250th Birthday
items.description: I Marine Expeditionary Force will host a live-fire Amphibious Capabilities Demonstration at Red Beach, Camp Pendleton, followed by a community Beach Bash at Del Mar Beach on Saturday, Oct. 18, 2025, to mark the Marine Corps’ 250th birthday and America’s Semiquincentennial.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Secretary Phelan Welcomes Under Secretary of the Navy Hung Cao and Launches Portfolio to Supercharge Warrior Ethos and Quality of Service Across the Navy and Marine Corps
items.description: Today, Secretary of the Navy John C. Phelan announced a wide-ranging cross departmental portfolio for the Under Secretary of the Navy (UNSECNAV) that unifies the Department’s most consequential levers for rebuilding warrior ethos and quality of service.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marine Corps Announces Project Dynamis to Accelerate AI-Powered Decision Advantage
items.description: The Assistant Commandant of the Marine Corps, Gen. Christopher J. Mahoney, signed a memorandum Sept. 10 to formally establish Project Dynamis, an initiative to accelerate the modernization of Marine Corps contributions to Combined Joint All-Domain Command and Control (CJADC2) in partnership with the Department of the Navy’s Project Overmatch.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marine Corps partners with Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Office and Defense Innovation Unit for Enterprise CJADC2 Capability Acceleration of Palantir System
items.description: The U.S. Marine Corps, in partnership with Defense Innovation Unit (DIU), the DoD Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Officer (CDAO), and Army Research Lab, finalized a contract with Palantir Technologies Inc. for an enterprise Marine Corps license for Maven Smart System (MSS), a foundational, data-centric command and control (C2) platform.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marines Pilot Artificial Intelligence Fellowship at NPS
items.description: U.S. Marines gathered at the Naval Postgraduate School (NPS) in Monterey, California, August 11–14,
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: America’s Rapid Response Force, Iwo Jima ARG-22nd MEU (SOC) Deploys for Global Operations
items.description: Sailors and Marines assigned to the Iwo Jima (IWO) Amphibious Ready Group (ARG) - 22nd Marine Expeditionary Unit (MEU) Special Operations Capable (SOC) departed for a regularly scheduled deployment, August 14.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: U.S. Marines Mobilize Without Delay: Shift from Exercise to Crisis Response
items.description: U.S. Marines postured around the globe serve as America’s rapid crisis response force, ready to meet the Nation’s needs at a moment’s notice. On July 26 Marine Corps readiness was on display, when U.S. Marine Medium Tiltrotor Squadron 363, operating under Marine Rotational Force–Darwin, deployed four MV-22B Ospreys more than 1,950 nautical miles from Darwin, Australia, to Clark Air Base, Philippines.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marines to Highlight Service Priorities, Future Challenges at Modern Day Marine 2025 Exposition
items.description: U.S. Marines will gather April 29 to May 1 at the Walter E. Washington Convention Center to present
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marine Corps Passes FY24 Financial Audit
items.description: For the second year in a row, independent auditors verified that the Marine Corps’ financial records are materially accurate, complete, and compliant with federal regulations and issued an unmodified opinion for Fiscal Year 2024.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Deputy Commandant for Aviation Unveils 2025 Aviation Plan, Reinforcing Future Readiness and Technological Integration
items.description: Headquarters Marine Corps Aviation released the 2025 Marine Corps Aviation Plan, outlining a
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Death of General Thomas R. Morgan, 21st Assistant Commandant of the Marine Corps
items.description: The Marine Corps announces the passing of the 21st Assistant Commandant of the Marine Corps, General Thomas R. Morgan on December 6, 2024.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marine Corps releases Talent Management Update, remains committed to empowering Marines while enhancing lethality
items.description: The Marine Corps released the latest Talent Management update in support of Force Design, highlighting the continuous efforts to modernize and innovate its talent management systems, enhancing readiness and empowering Marines to thrive in a dynamic operational environment. Building on the foundation set by Talent Management 2030 and the Talent Management Campaign Plan 2023-2025, Manpower and Reserve Affairs (M&RA) achieved significant milestones in retaining, developing, and optimizing the force.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: USMC/MOD Joint Statement - Commencement of Force Flow
items.description: A small detachment of approximately 100 logistics support Marines from III Marine Expeditionary Force (MEF) has started to relocate from Okinawa to Guam in line with the Joint Statement of the Security Consultative Committee (“2+2”) on July 28, 2024.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marine Corps crushes fiscal year 2024 end strength with historic retention, recruiting success
items.description: The Marine Corps achieved historic first-term reenlistment numbers and exceeded its retention goal for Fiscal Year 2024 in a competitive and challenging environment. Engaged leaders across the Marine Corps retained the most qualified Marines at levels not seen in over a decade. Additionally, these efforts contributed to the Marine Corps exceeding its authorized end strength requirement, which is a summation of recruiting and retention efforts.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: SECNAV Del Toro Reflects on Three Years in Office at the Future Warfighting Symposium
items.description: Secretary of the Navy Carlos Del Toro delivered remarks at the Future Warfighting Symposium at the Naval War College in Newport, R.I., Aug. 8. Secretary Del Toro addressed new Naval War College (NWC) students, faculty, and staff on the eve of his third anniversary in office.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: MARINE BARRACKS WASHINGTON TO HOST ENLISTED-LED EVENING PARADE
items.description: Marine Barracks Washington will host an enlisted-led Evening Parade at Marine Barracks Washington, D.C., this Friday, July 26 at 8:45 pm.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marine Corps launches Barracks Digital Maintenance Request System Service-Wide
items.description: The Marine Corps launched and has fully implemented QSRMax, which is the service’s main effort to upgrade its maintenance request system from analog to digital.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: CMC Signs the First Training and Education Annual Report on Force Design
items.description: U.S. Marine Corps Commandant General Eric M. Smith has signed the U.S. Marine Corps Training and Education Annual Report (TEAR), June, 12, 2024. Published and distributed by Commanding General U.S. Marine Corps Training and Education Command (TECOM) Lieutenant General Kevin M. Iiams, the TEAR is the first annual report since the publishing of Training and Education 2030 (TE2030) by General David H. Berger, the 38th Commandant of the Marine Corps. The 14-page document outlines how CG TECOM has organized, progressed, and accomplished the 37 Force Design directed actions from TE2030.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Funeral details for the late 29th Commandant of the Marine Corps, General Alfred M. Gray Jr.
items.description: On Monday, July 29, 2024, at 11:00 a.m., funeral services will be held at the Fort Myer Memorial Chapel for the 29th Commandant of the Marine Corps, General Alfred M. Gray Jr., and his wife, Mrs. Janice Gray. Immediately following funeral services, graveside full military honors will occur at Arlington National Cemetery to lay General and Mrs. Gray to rest.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Exercise Native Fury 24 begins in Saudi Arabia
items.description: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia (KSA), the United Arab Emirates, and U.S. Marine Corps Forces, Central Command (USMARCENT) launched Exercise NATIVE FURY 24 (NF 24) at a Saudi Naval Port on May 5, 2024.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marines Showcase Global Operations, New Tech at Modern Day Marine 2024 Exposition
items.description: U.S. Marines will gather next week at the Walter E. Washington Convention Center to present the latest in operating concepts, modernization efforts, and new technology innovations during the Marine Corps’ annual military exposition.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marine Corps publishes Marine Corps Warfighting Publication 8-10
items.description: The Marine Corps published its latest information doctrine publication Marine Corps Warfighting Publication 8-10, Information in Marine Corps Operations, March 14, 2024.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Death of General Alfred M. Gray, 29th Commandant of the Marine Corps
items.description: It is with great sadness that the Marine Corps announces the death of the 29th Commandant of the Marine Corps, General Alfred M. Gray. General Gray died March 20, 2024 in Alexandria, Virginia, after a brief stay in hospice care.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: 26th MEU(SOC) and BATARG to Return Home After an 8 Month Deployment
items.description: Marines and Sailors assigned to the 26th Marine Expeditionary Unit (Special Operations Capable) will return home in several waves beginning Mar. 16, 2024, after successfully completing an extended eight-month deployment embarked aboard the amphibious warships of the Bataan Amphibious Ready Group (BATARG) in U.S. Naval Forces Europe and Africa/6th Fleet and U.S. Naval Forces Central Command/5th Fleet areas of operations.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marine Corps Returns MV-22 to Flight Status
items.description: The Marine Corps returned its MV-22s to flight on March 8, following Naval Air Systems Command’s (NAVAIR) announcement that deemed the aircraft safe to fly.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Gen Smith Returns to Full Duty Status as Commandant
items.description: The Commandant of the Marine Corps, Gen. Eric M. Smith, has returned to full duty status after recovering from an October 29 cardiac arrest.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: CNO, ACMC Travel to Gulf Coast for Shipbuilding Visit
items.description: Chief of Naval Operations (CNO) Adm. Lisa Franchetti and Assistant Commandant of the Marine Corps (ACMC) Gen. Christopher J. Mahoney visited Austal USA, Ingalls Shipbuilding and Bollinger Mississippi Shipbuilding, March 4, 2024, demonstrating the Navy and Marine Corps full integration and commitment to delivering warfighting advantage.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marine Corps XQ-58A Valkyrie completes second successful flight
items.description: The Marine Corps’ XQ-58A Valkyrie, a highly autonomous, low-cost tactical unmanned air vehicle, successfully completed its second test flight February 23, 2024 at Eglin Air Force Base, Fla.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marine Corps Passes FY23 Audit
items.description: Following a rigorous two-year audit pilot, and for the first time in DoD history, the Marine Corps received an unmodified audit opinion, which is the highest audit opinion that can be achieved.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: U.S. Marines Deliver Emergency Relief to Mindanao
items.description: At the request of the Government of the Philippines, U.S. Marines from III Marine Expeditionary Force will support the U.S. Agency for International Development by providing foreign humanitarian assistance to the ongoing disaster relief mission in Mindanao, Republic of the Philippines, Feb. 12, 2024.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marine Corps orders environmental, health, and safety inspections of all service barracks
items.description: Effective immediately, Marine Corps Installations Command will conduct an environmental, health, and safety inspection of all Marine Corps barracks to ensure service compliance with its commitment to its residents to provide a safe, secure, clean, and consistent living standard across the unaccompanied housing enterprise.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marine Corps Provides Update on General Smith
items.description: The Commandant of the Marine Corps, Gen. Eric. M. Smith, underwent successful open heart surgery on Jan. 8 to repair a bicuspid aortic valve in his heart, which was the cause of his cardiac arrest on Oct. 29.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: V-22 Osprey Grounding
items.description: Out of an abundance of caution, following the AFSOC operational stand down, NAVAIR is instituting a grounding bulletin for all V-22 Osprey variants Dec. 6. This decision comes after the V-22 Osprey mishap on Nov. 29, off the shore of Yakushima, Japan.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marine Corps Provides 4th Update on Gen. Smith
items.description: The Commandant of the Marine Corps, Gen. Eric. M. Smith, was released from inpatient care on Nov. 15 and is continuing his recovery.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: 12th Marine Regiment Redesignates to 12th Marine Littoral Regiment
items.description: 12th Marine Regiment redesignated to 12th Marine Littoral Regiment (MLR) on November 15, 2023,
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Secretary of the Navy Remarks as Prepared, Arlington Cemetery Wreath Laying, Nov. 10.
items.description: Secretary of the Navy (SECNAV) Carlos Del Toro delivers remarks during a wreath laying ceremony celebrating the 248th birthday of the United States Marine Corps.
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Commandant Transitions to Inpatient Rehabilitation
items.description: General Smith continues to make progress as he continues on his path to full recovery. Based on his
items.author: Press Operations

items.title: Marine Corps Provides 2nd Update on Gen. Smith
items.description: General Smith is making excellent progress following sudden cardiac arrest earlier this week. His
items.author: Press Operations
